title: Regulación de redes sociales y de IA: Estos son los temas primordiales para Morena en el Congreso
summary: El grupo parlamentario de Morena en la Cámara de Diputados definió sus prioridades legislativas para el próximo periodo ordinario, entre las que destaca la regulación de las redes sociales y del uso de la inteligencia artificial. El documento marca la agenda de gobernanza de IA que el Congreso mexicano abordará en los próximos meses.

title: México: furor por los fondos de inversión
summary: Los fondos de inversión en México registran un dinamismo extraordinario desde la pandemia, con cifras que marcan hitos notables y que, según el análisis, podrían duplicarse en los próximos años. La columna describe una tendencia estructural y no un repunte pasajero en el ahorro e inversión de los mexicanos.

title: Nu ya operará como banco en México; recibió autorización de la CNBV
summary: Nu México, filial del gigante brasileño de servicios financieros digitales, recibió autorización final de la CNBV para operar como banco, sumándose a los 54 bancos que operan en el país. Es el más reciente ejemplo de un jugador digital que da el salto a banca plena en el mercado mexicano.

title: El Mundial dejó una prueba de estrés para los pagos digitales; deben ser más resilientes
summary: La Copa Mundial 2026 evidenció que, en eventos de alta concentración de personas, el éxito de los pagos digitales depende menos de la terminal en el comercio que de la solidez de toda la infraestructura tecnológica detrás. Directivos de tecnología de pagos como CLAI Payments piden reforzar la resiliencia del ecosistema mexicano de pagos.

title: La inflación que el Banco de México no puede controlar
summary: En junio la inflación anual bajó a 3.37%, su nivel más bajo desde diciembre de 2020, pero el dato mensual bueno no borra los riesgos de fondo. La columna advierte sobre presiones de largo plazo, ligadas al cambio climático, que escapan al control directo de Banxico y de la política monetaria convencional.

title: La tokenización puede transformar la arquitectura del sistema financiero mundial, según el FMI
summary: Un blog del FMI firmado por Tobias Adrian sostiene que la tokenización de activos financieros tiene el potencial de cambiar la arquitectura del sistema financiero global, aunque el resultado final dependerá de las decisiones regulatorias y de política pública que se tomen a partir de ahora.

title: TSMC Sales Surge 36% in Fresh Sign of AI Spending Momentum
summary: TSMC reported quarterly sales up 36%, beating expectations and signaling that global demand for AI hardware remains intact despite broader market jitters. The results reinforce the view that enterprise AI infrastructure spending is still accelerating.

title: AI is not enough to arrest China's decline
summary: The piece argues that AI adoption alone cannot offset the more powerful structural forces weighing on China's economy, pushing back on the narrative that AI investment will single-handedly reverse the country's economic slowdown.

title: Companies turn to Chinese AI models to cut costs
summary: DoorDash, Siemens and Airbnb are among the companies turning to Chinese AI models to curb ballooning bills and reduce reliance on US technology providers, a sign that enterprise AI adoption decisions are increasingly driven by cost and vendor diversification, not just capability.

title: Wall Street feasts on fees from SpaceX IPO and mega-mergers
summary: Top US banks are benefiting from a revival of large dealmaking, with fee income boosted by the SpaceX IPO and a wave of mega-mergers, as AI optimism and resilient consumer spending fuel a broader market rally.

title: Jane Fraser's ruthless remake of Citigroup
summary: The piece profiles Citigroup CEO Jane Fraser's turnaround effort, which has involved asset sales, job cuts and controversial new hires alongside a closer relationship with Trump, asking whether the overhaul is enough to restore the bank's standing among its top-tier peers.

title: Employers pushed staff to use AI more. That has backfired
summary: Companies that pushed employees hard to adopt AI tools are now facing backlash as the costs of those tools start to bite, forcing a rethink of incentive structures around AI usage. The piece is a cautionary case study in how workplace AI adoption programs can go wrong once budget pressure hits.

title: Storm clouds gather over America's financial supremacy
summary: The piece warns that America's dominance in global finance is facing mounting pressure, with US payments firms flagged as potentially the first casualties of an eroding financial supremacy. It frames this as an emerging systemic risk for the broader financial system.

title: Apple's M6, M7 and M8 Chips Show How AI Is Reshaping the Company
summary: A rundown of Apple's upcoming M6, M7 and M8 chip lineup illustrates how AI is reshaping the company's hardware roadmap, alongside plans for new Apple Pencils.

title: Zhipu's Chinese Founder Says Frontier AI Should Stay Open to All
summary: The founder of Chinese AI lab Zhipu argued that frontier artificial intelligence should remain broadly accessible rather than controlled by a select few, weighing in on the growing debate over how to govern increasingly powerful AI models.

title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over AI Talent Fight
summary: Apple has sued OpenAI, alleging that former employees who left for OpenAI's hardware division improperly took trade secrets, reflecting an intensifying battle for AI talent and device leadership between the two companies.

title: Architecture in the Andes: How Altitude Shapes Design Decisions
summary: The piece explores how altitude and shifting microclimates across the Andes — from páramos to snow-covered peaks — shape architectural design decisions in Ecuador, Peru, Bolivia, Colombia and Chile, offering a broader look at how mountain terrain informs residential design.
